---
title: "Glossary"
---

Key terms used across the book. Each links back to the chapter where it first appears.


### capital per effective worker _(also: k-tilde)_

Capital divided by labor measured in efficiency units, k̃ = K/(AL). The variable that converges in Solow. — [↪ first used in solow_intro](content/solow_intro.html)

### depreciation

The fraction of the capital stock that wears out each period, denoted δ. — [↪ first used in solow_intro](content/solow_intro.html)

### diminishing returns

Each additional unit of capital adds less output than the last — the force that pulls the economy to a steady state. — [↪ first used in cobb_d_prod](content/cobb_d_prod.html)

### savings rate _(also: s)_

The constant fraction s of output that households save and firms invest each period. — [↪ first used in solow_intro](content/solow_intro.html)

### steady state _(also: k*, balanced growth path)_

The level where a variable stops changing over time. In Solow, where investment exactly offsets depreciation + dilution, so k̇ = 0. — [↪ first used in solow_intro](content/solow_intro.html)

### total factor productivity _(also: TFP, A)_

The efficiency term A multiplying the production function; its growth rate g sets long-run per-capita growth. — [↪ first used in solow_intro](content/solow_intro.html)
